Create address-pr-comments skill: 自动处理和回复 PR comments

- 读取 PR review comments 并验证有效性
- LLM 驱动的 comment 验证和修复建议

- examples/invalid-replies.md - Invalid comment 回复示例
- README.md - 快速开始指南

回复格式：
- Fixed: "Fixed in <hash>: <description>"
- Invalid: 礼貌说明原因，邀请讨论

fix: raise RPC timeout for web.login.wait long-poll

The default 30s RPC timeout is too short for QR login long-polling.
Set the RPC timeout to serverPollMs + 15s headroom so the gateway
has time to respond before the RPC client aborts. Restore Panel poll
timeout to 60s now that the RPC layer won't cut it short.

fix: reduce QR poll timeout below RPC 30s limit and retry on errors

GatewayRpcClient has a 30s request timeout. The 60s poll timeout
caused the RPC to abort before the server could respond. Reduce to
25s and treat poll errors as transient (retry until session deadline)
instead of immediately showing "Connection failed".

fix: bridge sessionKey between loginWithQrStart and loginWithQrWait

OpenClaw's web.login.wait gateway handler only forwards { timeoutMs,
accountId } to the plugin, dropping sessionKey. The upstream weixin
plugin needs sessionKey to poll for QR scan completion.

Fix by capturing sessionKey from loginWithQrStart at module scope and
injecting it into loginWithQrWait params when missing. This makes the
QR modal detect successful scans and close properly.

original minimax config from the yaml file.

the default agent entry to match. CLI flags still override all agents;

fix(web): prevent skill card drag from opening a second window (#361)

* fix(controller): seed config writer cache from disk on cold start

On process restart, lastWrittenContent is null, causing the first write()
to always hit disk even if the config file already has identical content.
This triggers an unnecessary OpenClaw reload on every controller restart.

Read the existing file to seed the in-memory cache on the first write()
call, so cold starts skip the write when content matches.

fix: ResolveProvider now searches agents list when no providers block exists

When relay.provider names a provider (e.g. kimi) but there is no
providers: block, ResolveProvider was returning only the provider name
with empty APIKey and Model. The default agent then filled those gaps
with its own model (MiniMax-M2.5), causing wrong model to be used.

Now ResolveProvider searches agents by ID or provider type as step 4,
returning the full config (key + model) from the matching agent entry.

fix(app): handle EIO error in system resume console.log

Wrap console.log in try-catch within the powerMonitor 'resume' handler.
After system sleep/hibernate, the PTY/console socket may be in a broken
state, causing console.log to throw 'write EIO' through electron-log's
transport pipeline.

Also silence the .catch handler to avoid secondary console.error EIO
when cron recovery fails for the same reason.

fix: include agentId in text.delta WS events to prevent cross-agent streaming bleed

sendStreamDelta() was emitting text.delta without agentId, so the
client-side filter (packetAgentId !== agentId) never triggered.
When switching agents mid-stream, the new agent's view would show
the previous agent's streaming content.

Changes:
- send.ts: add optional agentId param to sendStreamDelta, include in WS payload
- reply-dispatcher.ts: pass agentId to both sendStreamDelta call sites

errors from readBuiltinResource but re-threw them, causing unhandled
promise rejections reported to Sentry (ELECTRON-68, 1743 events).

Return empty string on failure instead, consistent with how
readAssistantResource already handles missing files.

Full WASM pipeline for VNC framebuffer decoding

Architecture change: WASM now owns the RGBA framebuffer surface.
All rect encodings (Raw, CopyRect, RRE, Hextile, ZRLE) write
directly to the WASM-owned surface in linear memory, eliminating
per-rect allocation and JS/WASM boundary copies on output.

Pipeline:
- initFramebuffer() allocates once on display-init / resize
- process{Raw,CopyRect,Rre,Hextile,Zrle}() write in-place
- getFramebuffer() returns zero-copy Uint8ClampedArray view
- JS still h

fix: merge peerServer into app — single port for all gateway routes (#130)

Closes #129

## What

Removes the separate `peerServer` Fastify instance (port 8099) and
registers all `/peer/*` routes directly on `app` (port 8100).

## Why

`GATEWAY_URL` is a single base URL used by both `announceToGateway()`
(`POST /peer/announce`) and `list_worlds()` (`GET /worlds`). With two
servers on different ports, `/peer/announce` was unreachable via the
public HTTPS domain.

- 优雅关闭（SIGTERM → 5s → SIGKILL）
- 支持 Linux systemd 和 macOS launchd 开机自启

fix: don't blacklist session on failed delete (#30)

When sessions.delete is rejected by the gateway (e.g. "webchat clients
cannot delete sessions"), the session was still added to the local
blacklist in localStorage, making it permanently invisible until the
user manually clears site data.

Now we return early on delete failure so the session remains visible
in the sidebar.

* fix(session): add commit lock to prevent concurrent re-commit race

commit_async() now acquires an asyncio.Lock during Phase 1 (copy +
clear + file write). This prevents concurrent commits from re-committing
the same messages. The lock is released before the slow LLM summary and
memory extraction, so it doesn't block other operations.

summary is g

Fix: switch approval service from getUpdates polling to file-based approval

getUpdates conflicted with OpenClaw's bot polling — approval replies were
consumed by OpenClaw before the approval service could see them.

New flow: write pending file -> send Telegram notification (one-way) ->
poll file for status change -> user runs oc-approve CLI or sends "approve
apv-XXX" via OpenClaw.

Added: bin/oc-approve.py (Windows CLI), wsl/oc-approve (WSL wrapper)

fix: use subagent_spawning hook to enforce cheap model for background tasks

api.runtime.subagent.run() silently ignores the `model` param
(not in SubagentRunParams type). The hook intercepts subagent creation
and sets modelOverride for all qmemory:subagent:* sessions.

Saves ~351K tokens/session by routing dedup/extract/link to zai/glm-5
instead of inheriting the parent session's expensive primary model.

fix: never permanently disable embeddings — check on every call (v0.3.15) (#141)

The retry loop (10 × 500ms) was too short for model download + load.
Once it timed out, embeddings were permanently disabled for the session.
All subsequent writes stored memories without vectors.

Fix: remove the retry/state machine entirely. Each getEmbedding() call
just tries hfe.embed() — if the model isn't ready yet, returns null.
Once Harper finishes loading the model, the next call succeeds.
No permanent fai
fix: prefer local models and keep modelscope progress

refactor: 에러코드 종류별 세분화 및 원인 체인 표시 (#101)

기존 INVALID_REQUEST(83종 에러를 단일 코드로 표기) / UNAVAILABLE(30종 에러를 단일 코드로 표기)를
구체적인 에러 종류별 코드로 분리:

INVALID_REQUEST →
- MISSING_PARAM: 필수 파라미터 누락
- NOT_FOUND: 리소스(세션/에이전트/노드 등) 미존재
- UNAUTHORIZED: 인증 실패/권한 부족
- VALIDATION_FAILED: 파라미터 값/스키마 유효성 위반
- CONFLICT: 리소스 상태 충돌(중복 생성, ID 불일치 등)
- FORBIDDEN: 정책에 의한 차단(세션 정책, 예약어 등)

UNAVAILABLE →
- NODE_DISCONNECTED: 대상 노드 WebSocket 연결 끊김
- DEPENDENCY_FAILED: 의존 서비스(TTS/브라우저 등) 장애
- FEATURE_DISABLED: 기능 비활성화 상태

- On Windows: reads from Windows Certificate Store
- On macOS: reads from Security.framework
- On Linux: reads from system cert paths

    - 有时左边明显，有时右边明显
    - 静态还好，一动起来更明显

    - 为什么一边脸看起来更大

* fix(controller): seed config writer cache from disk on cold start

On process restart, lastWrittenContent is null, causing the first write()
to always hit disk even if the config file already has identical content.
This triggers an unnecessary OpenClaw reload on every controller restart.

Read the existing file to seed the in-memory cache on the first write()
call, so cold starts skip the write when content matches.

fix: auto theme does not switch when OS changes day/night mode (#6813) (#12278)

When theme is set to "auto" (follow system), changing the OS from
light to dark (or vice versa) without reloading the page had no
effect. The theme was only evaluated once on page load.

Added a matchMedia 'change' event listener that calls switchTheme()
whenever the OS color scheme changes, but only when theme is "auto".
Listener is properly cleaned up on component unmount.

Fixes #6813 (point 1)

the model can't be downloaded without knowing where to store it.

fix(agent): JSON validation hook 增加 PostToolUse 兜底和诊断日志 (#136)

PreToolUse 模拟式验证存在结构性盲区——当 old_string 不匹配或转义
层级差异导致 json.loads(simulated) 放行时，无效编辑会直接写入文件。

fix: expand tilde (~) in vision_analyze local file paths (#2585)

Path('~/.hermes/image.png').is_file() returns False because Path
doesn't expand tilde. This caused the tool to fall through to URL
validation, which also failed, producing a confusing error:
'Invalid image source. Provide an HTTP/HTTPS URL or a valid local
file path.'

Fix: use os.path.expanduser() before constructing the Path object.
Added two tests for tilde expansion (success and nonexistent file).

feat: auto-reconnect failed gateway platforms with exponential backoff (#2584)

When a messaging platform fails to connect at startup (e.g. transient DNS
failure) or disconnects at runtime with a retryable error, the gateway now
queues it for background reconnection instead of giving up permanently.

- New _platform_reconnect_watcher background task runs alongside the
  existing session expiry watcher
- Exponential backoff: 30s, 60s, 120s, 240s, 300s cap
- Max 20 retry attempts before giving up

fix(agent-bench): strip openrouter/ prefix when estimating cost from pricing map

OpenClaw session JSONL files store model names with the openrouter/
prefix (e.g. openrouter/z-ai/glm-5-turbo), but the OpenRouter pricing
API keys models without it (z-ai/glm-5-turbo). This caused the pricing
lookup to fail silently, leaving cost at $0.00 for models whose
providers don't natively report usage.cost.total.

fix(e2e): fix interactive E2E test chain (provision → install → input test) (#2898)

* fix(e2e): pass SPAWN_NAME + SPAWN_ENABLED_STEPS to interactive harness

Without SPAWN_NAME, cmdRun prompts 'Name your spawn' interactively.
The AI driver (Claude Haiku) can't respond because ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN
is an OpenRouter key — every Anthropic API call returns 401, so the harness
returns <wait> indefinitely until the 20-min SESSION_TIMEOUT_MS fires.

SPAWN_ENABLED_STEPS=auto-update bypasses the setup op

fix: Cloudflare DNS step error visibility and idempotency (#125)

## Problem

The `Update Cloudflare DNS` step fails with exit code 22 (curl `-f` flag
silently discards the CF API error body), blocking every deploy on
`main` push.

## Fix

- Remove `-f` from curl so Cloudflare API errors are visible in logs
- Check `success == true` in the API response; downgrade failures to
`::warning::` so deploys are not blocked
- Skip the update when DNS already points to the correct IP (idempotent
— importa

fix(gateway): use /health instead of /api/health for health probes (#481)

The gateway health probe in `buildGatewayProbeUrl()` hardcodes `/api/health`
as the probe endpoint, but the OpenClaw gateway serves its health check on
`/health`. This causes the server-side health probe to always return HTTP 404,
marking gateways as offline/error even when they are healthy and responsive.

Changed the probe endpoint from `/api/health` to `/health` in:
- `src/app/api/gateways/health/route.ts` (buildGatewa

fix: use EC2_PUBLIC_IP variable instead of DescribeInstances (#124)

## Problem

The deploy workflow fails with:
```
UnauthorizedOperation: arn:aws:sts::...:assumed-role/awn-gateway-deploy/GitHubActions is not authorized to perform: ec2:DescribeInstances
```

The `awn-gateway-deploy` IAM role used by CI does not have
`ec2:DescribeInstances` permission.

## Fix

Read `EC2_IP` from a GitHub Actions **variable** `EC2_PUBLIC_IP` instead
of calling the AWS API. Fails fast with a clear error message i

containing team name (e.g. [RaPDS]) instead of the assigned project name
(e.g. [Heartbeat-Gateway]). This caused the LLM to misclassify events
with generic titles because the project label in SOUL.md never matched.

project is null or absent. Handles the null-project case with `or {}`
guard

fix(on-complete): replace ${BASH_COMMAND@Q} with ${BASH_COMMAND} for bash 3.2 compat

macOS ships bash 3.2 which does not support the @Q parameter transform.
The ERR trap using ${BASH_COMMAND@Q} caused a bad substitution error on
every task completion, crashing on-complete.sh before it could fire
openclaw system event or send Telegram notifications.

Root cause of all missed completion notifications.

Fix terminal: use binary WebSocket frames for SSH data

Raw SSH terminal output contains bytes that corrupt UTF-8 text frames,
causing "Invalid frame header" errors. Now:
- Server sends SSH stdout/stderr as binary WebSocket frames (raw Buffer)
- Client uses binaryType='arraybuffer' and TextDecoder with fatal:false
- JSON text frames still used for control messages (status, error, exit)

实现了各阶段 Claude Code 触发函数：

- trigger_checklist_review() - 验收清单评审
  - 分析验收标准
  - 优化验收清单
  - 在 Issue 下回复结果

- trigger_tdd_red() - TDD RED
  - 编写验收测试
  - 运行测试确认失败
  - 提交代码

- trigger_design_review() - 技术方案评审
  - 设计技术方案
  - 输出技术方案文档
  - 在 Issue 下回复结果

- trigger_tdd_green() - TDD GREEN
  - 实现功能
  - 运行测试确认通过
  - 提交代码

- trigger_tdd_refactor() - TDD REFACTOR
  - 代码重构优化
  - 运行测试确认通过
  - 提交代码

- trigger_final_review() - 最终评审
  - 验收清单确认
  - 代码质量确认
  - 生成最终评审报告

apply to NEW prompts arriving after previous approval, not the just-approved

fix(e2e): fix _stage_prompt_remotely to embed prompt inline instead of stdin pipe (#2897)

The stdin piping approach was broken: _hetzner_exec runs remote commands via
"printf '%s' 'ENCODED_CMD' | base64 -d | bash", which connects bash's stdin to
the base64 pipe rather than SSH's outer stdin. So `cat > /tmp/.e2e-prompt` read
from EOF — the encoded prompt was never written to the remote file.

Fix: embed the validated base64 prompt directly in the command string using
printf. This is safe because

fix(gateway): prevent panic on send to closed WebSocket client channel

Agent run goroutines can outlive the WebSocket client (e.g. after
reconnect). When the goroutine finishes and calls SendResponse/SendEvent,
the closed send channel causes a panic that crashes the server.

Add defer/recover to both methods to gracefully drop messages for
disconnected clients instead of panicking.

context deadline exceeded errors (~210/hr) when Telegram held the
long-poll for the full duration. Increase HTTP client to 60s, reduce
getUpdates to 25s for 35s headroom.

refactor: rename p2p_ agent tools to awn_ prefix (#119)

## Summary

The `p2p_` prefix was inherited from the old DAP plugin and implies
generic peer-to-peer, which is misleading — these tools are
AWN-specific:
- Peer discovery is world-scoped (peers only visible after `join_world`)
- Messages are signed with the AWN protocol
- Status reflects AWN identity and joined worlds

## Changes

| Old name | New name |
|---|---|
| `p2p_send_message` | `awn_send_message` |
| `p2p_list_peers` | `awn_list_p

Fix chat timeout: support synchronous chat.send and add diagnostic logging

The gateway auto-delivers tick/health events but NOT chat events, and
events.subscribe is not supported. This means waitForChat() will always
time out.

New approach:
- Give chat.send the full RESPONSE_TIMEOUT (120s) instead of 30s, since
  the gateway may return the AI response synchronously in the res payload
- Check sendResult for an inline response before falling back to events
- Add detailed logging of what chat.sen

fix(traceflow): 修复会话详情加载 bug，改用行数判断而非文件大小

## 问题
原逻辑使用文件大小（500KB）判断是否用 head_tail 模式，导致：
- 500KB 但只有 100 行的文件（大 JSON）被错误地用 head_tail
- 用户点击会话详情看不到完整消息

## 修复
改用行数判断（500 行阈值）：
1. 先读取文件头部 64KB，估算平均行大小
2. 计算总行数 = 文件大小 / 平均行大小
3. 行数 <= 500 → 全量读取
4. 行数 > 500 → head_tail 模式

## 影响
- 小文件（<500 行）无论大小都全量读取
- 大文件（>500 行）才用首尾分片
- 确保消息详情页正常加载

* chore: update agent GitHub star counts

* fix(qa): load ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN as ANTHROPIC_API_KEY for interactive E2E

QA VMs store the Anthropic key as ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN in
/etc/spawn-qa-auth.env, but the e2e-interactive handler only looked for
ANTHROPIC_API_KEY — causing the 6am cron to fail immediately with
"ANTHROPIC_API_KEY not set". Accept either name when loading from the
auth env file.

- Windows UTF8 encoding writes BOM by default; serde_json fails with 'expected value
  at line 1 column 1'. Fixed by writing result with UTF8NoBOM and stripping BOM in
  parse_result() as a safety net.
- regsvr32/reg/etc do not set LASTEXITCODE inside & { } 2>&1 blocks. Fixed by writing
  the user command to a separate inner .ps1 file and running it via Sta

Query active sessions before roster inject instead of hardcoding 'main'

chat.inject requires an existing session key. The hardcoded 'main'
doesn't match the actual session key format used by OpenClaw gateways
(e.g. agent:main:telegram:direct:...). Now queries sessions.list first
and injects into the first active session found.

fix(command-center): BUG-001 — add retry loop to SSE fallback getTaskResult

Replace single getTaskResult fetch with a 4-attempt retry loop (2s intervals).
When SSE ends without a terminal event (network hiccup, timeout), the fallback
now polls until the task reaches a terminal status (completed/failed/cancelled)
or exhausts all retries before surfacing an error message.

fix(slack): comprehensive nil pointer protection for all handlers

Problem Discovery:
- Deep investigation revealed handleViewSubmission also has nil pointer risk
- 3 unprotected calls: http.Error(), w.Header().Set(), w.WriteHeader()
- Future risk: Socket Mode modal support would trigger these panics

Root Cause Analysis:
- Socket Mode passes nil as http.ResponseWriter (no HTTP response needed)
- Multiple handlers assumed ResponseWriter is always present
- Pattern repeated in 7 different handler

feat(traceflow-deploy): 智能检测服务状态，优化重启流程

## 改动

修改 deploy-pm2.sh 脚本，增加服务状态检测：

### 服务存活时（pm2 online）
- 直接执行 build
- 然后 restart（不检查端口占用）
- 适用场景：代码更新、日常重启

### 服务未存活时
- 执行完整部署流程
- 严格检查端口占用
- 适用场景：首次部署、服务崩溃后恢复

## 优势

1. **更快重启**：服务存活时跳过端口检查
2. **更安全**：服务未存活时严格检查端口
3. **更清晰**：日志明确区分两种模式

Set localhost Origin header on WebSocket connections

Docker-networked bridge containers connect from non-localhost IPs,
causing gateway controlUi origin checks to reject the connection.
Setting Origin to http://localhost:<port> passes the origin allowlist.

Combined with gateway-side config (dangerouslyDisableDeviceAuth,
allowInsecureAuth, allowedOrigins including localhost), this allows
the bridge to connect from Docker containers without implementing
the WebCrypto device identity protocol.

fix: gstack-slug bash compatibility — source to eval (#354)

* fix: replace source <(gstack-slug) with eval for bash compatibility

Under bash with set -euo pipefail, source <(cmd) process substitution
doesn't reliably set variables in the caller's scope. The variables
stay empty and -u (nounset) crashes the script. eval "$(cmd)" works
correctly in both bash and zsh.

Before this PR:

`pdf-parse@2.4.5` depends on `@napi-rs/canvas@0.1.80`, which does not
include Windows ARM64 support. This causes issues for Windows ARM64
users.

After this PR:

includes Windows ARM64 support.

Set Origin header on WebSocket to match gateway host

The gateway enforces controlUi.allowedOrigins on WebSocket connections.
The bridge running in Docker sends no Origin header (or a container-
internal one), causing rejection. Set Origin to http://<gateway-host>:<port>
so it matches the gateway's own address and passes the origin check.

fix: clamp inf/nan similarity scores at source to prevent JSON crash (#882)

The C++ vector engine can produce infinity values from inner product
overflow on non-normalized vectors (distance_type=ip with
NormalizeVector=False). These inf scores propagate through the adapter
and retriever layers, ultimately crashing JSON serialization with
ValueError.

Fix applied at two layers:

1. CollectionAdapter.query() — clamp non-finite scores to 0.0
   immediately after reading from the C++ engine result,

fix: skip disabled providers in combo fallback instead of returning 406 (#336)

When a provider has credentials but all are disabled, return 404 (NOT_FOUND)
instead of 400 (BAD_REQUEST). The combo handler already treats 404 as a
fallbackable error, so it will skip to the next model in the chain.

Previously, the 400 status caused the combo to stop with a hard error,
killing the client (e.g., Claude Code) even though other models in the
combo chain were available.

修复: Web 消息回复泄漏到飞书 + SDK 后置异常导致进程崩溃

1. IM 会话中 IPC 注入 Web 消息后 replySourceImJid 清为 null，
   但 directImReply 仍为 true，导致回复仍发到飞书。
   新增 routeCleared 条件跳过 IM 发送；流式卡片回退到 web JID
   触发 dispose，避免继续向飞书推送流式内容。

2. SDK 在 yield success result 后再抛异常时，runQuery catch 块
   无条件 re-throw 导致 exit(1) + 退避重试。新增 resultCount > 0
   检查，已有成功结果时降级为非致命警告。

fix(slack): add nil check for http.ResponseWriter in interactive handlers

Problem:
- Panic: nil pointer dereference at interactive.go:339
- Occurred when Socket Mode called handleNewPermissionCallback with nil ResponseWriter
- Caused all subsequent messages to be unresponsive

Root Cause:
- Socket Mode flow passes nil as http.ResponseWriter (no HTTP response needed)
- Handlers directly called w.WriteHeader() without nil check
- Panic crashed the Socket Mode event loop, blocking all future inter

fix: normalize finish_reason to 'tool_calls' when tool calls are present (#379)

Some upstream providers (e.g. Antigravity) return non-standard finish_reason
values like 'other' instead of the OpenAI-standard 'tool_calls' when the
model invokes tools. This causes downstream consumers (e.g. OpenClaw) to
fail to execute tool calls, breaking agentic sub-agent workflows.

Changes:
- nonStreamingHandler: post-translation guard that normalizes finish_reason
  to 'tool_calls' when message.tool_calls is

fix: treat Kiro 400 'improperly formed request' as model-unavailable (#386)

Kiro returns HTTP 400 with 'Improperly formed request (reset after Xs)'
when a model is not available on that account's subscription tier.
Previously this fell through to COOLDOWN_MS.transient (30s), causing
rapid retries on all accounts before failing — all accounts get locked
simultaneously with no actual fallback.

Treating this as paymentRequired (2min cooldown) ensures:
1. The model is locked on that account for 2m

fix(chat): pick last non-empty message for Codex Responses SSE

Root cause: Codex/OpenAI Responses streams multiple alternating reasoning and
message output items. The first message block often has empty output_text; the
visible answer lives in a later message. Previous code used output.find() which
always picked the first (empty) message block.

Fix: walk message items from end and use the last message whose extracted text
is non-empty; fall back to final message if all are empty.

2. `flair memory add` POSTed to /Memory (405) instead of PUT /Memory/{id}

fix: 修复 CLAUDE_EXIT 初始化bug，npx成功时不再误判为失败

根因：CLAUDE_EXIT=1 初始化后，npx成功(exit=0)时 || 不触发，
CLAUDE_EXIT保持为1，导致成功执行被误判为失败进入容灾链路。

修复：在每次npx调用前重置 CLAUDE_EXIT=0，替代原来无效的
${CLAUDE_EXIT:-0} 兜底逻辑。

Fix code/open commands to work from IDLE state (headsup display)

The AI mode commands (code/open) previously required data.isFinal,
which meant they only worked on final transcriptions. This caused
issues when the headsup display was shown (IDLE state) because
interim transcriptions would be ignored and finals might not arrive.

Now the commands work on interim transcriptions too (like clear/hey),
with tracking to skip the matching final and avoid double-triggering.

fix(security): eliminate username enumeration timing side-channel (#1525)

When the username didn't match, verify_dashboard_password() returned
Denied immediately without performing Argon2id verification. This
created a measurable timing difference (~tens of ms) that allowed
attackers to enumerate valid usernames.

Now the password verification path always executes regardless of
whether the username matched, making the timing indistinguishable
between wrong-username and wrong-password cases.

fix: check linear-signature header (not x-linear-signature)

Linear sends the HMAC signature in the linear-signature header.
The gateway was checking x-linear-signature, which is always absent,
causing every Linear webhook to return 401 once a secret was configured.

The no-secret path (return True) masked this in early testing before
secrets were set. Added linear-signature as primary lookup with
x-linear-signature as fallback. Tests updated to use the correct header.
